artifact_type: hospital AI triage and care-routing governance report
working_title: The Clinical Flow Governor: Safe AI Triage, Escalation, and Throughput Control in a Capacity-Constrained Hospital
scenario_brief: A multi-hospital system is evaluating an AI-assisted triage and care-routing control plane for emergency department intake, inpatient bed assignment, imaging prioritization, specialist escalation, and discharge coordination. The system is under capacity stress, has variable EHR data quality, and must avoid unsafe automation, hidden bias, and false reassurance.
artifact_goal: Produce a client-shareable governance and implementation report that explains how to deploy AI triage support safely as an operations control plane without claiming autonomous clinical diagnosis or treatment authority.
complex_tensions: ED throughput versus missed high-acuity patients
bed-capacity optimization versus clinical deterioration risk
false negatives, false positives, and escalation fatigue
bias across language, disability, socioeconomic, and access factors
FDA, malpractice, HIPAA, audit, and quality-review boundaries
handoff failures between ED, inpatient, radiology, specialists, and discharge teams
hidden_failure_targets: artifact treats AI triage as diagnosis
artifact ignores escalation thresholds and clinician authority
artifact optimizes queue metrics while hiding safety failures
artifact fails to separate observed clinical data from model inference
artifact lacks audit schema for overrides, delays, and adverse events
source_pack_needed: recent ED boarding or hospital capacity source
clinical AI safety or FDA guidance source
HIPAA or health data privacy source
bias/equity in triage or clinical algorithms source
EHR interoperability or data quality source
domain_specific_disclaimer: This artifact is not medical advice, not a clinical diagnosis, and not a substitute for licensed clinician judgment.
